title: Concurrency Budgets, Backpressure, Rate Limits, and Worker Threads · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A production review compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for concurrency budgets, backpressure, rate limits, and worker threads and demands the smallest safe admission, concurrency, queue, rate, and CPU-work budget with changed-input evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for admission, concurrency, queue, rate, and CPU-work budget. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish asynchronous concurrency from parallelism and measure CPU blocking, synchronous I/O, long callbacks, and event-loop delay.
Bound active requests, per-dependency concurrency, queue length, queue age, memory, rejection, Retry-After, and fairness separately.
Apply rate and quota policies to trusted identities, tenant and operation cost, distributed storage, clock behavior, headers, exemptions, and failure mode.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Event Loop Lag Blocking · changed case · Quiz Event Loop Lag Blocking
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz team handles case 7280 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to combine concurrency, queue, tenant-rate, and bounded CPU-offload decisions; record only bounded redacted diagnostic fields; then change the method and preserve both status and durable-effect evidence. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Distinguish asynchronous concurrency from parallelism and measure CPU blocking, synchronous I/O, long callbacks, and event-loop delay.

Which action demonstrates Event Loop Lag Blocking without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Node and Express server Event Loop Lag Blocking · changed case · Quiz Event Loop Lag Blocking.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish asynchronous concurrency from parallelism and measure cpu blocking, synchronous i/o, long callbacks, and event-loop delay. For Node and Express server Event Loop Lag Blocking · changed case · Quiz Event Loop Lag Blocking, keep Event Loop Lag Blocking connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.
